evals.prompt: I keep writing release notes by hand from git log output. Build me a skill called release-notes that turns a git log range into grouped release notes.
evals.expected_output: A new release-notes skill directory with SKILL.md, mise.toml, a CI workflow, scripts, tests under scripts/tests, references, assets, and evals, plus a green mise run ci and a PASS from the validator, reported with the fresh command output.
assertions: A directory named release-notes exists with SKILL.md at its root and frontmatter opening at byte 0.
mise run ci inside release-notes exits 0 and the reply quotes the run.
evals/evals.json in release-notes holds at least 4 cases with assertions.
No file in release-notes names an agent product or a model.

evals.prompt: Can you check whether the skill at ./skills/pdf-tools meets the agent skills contract? It was written by hand last month.
evals.expected_output: A validation report listing each contract check as PASS or FAIL, with file and reason for every failure and a proposed fix per failure.
assertions: The reply shows output from validate_skill.py, lint_writing.py, and check_code_rules.py.
Every FAIL line carries a file reference and a reason.
Each reported failure comes with a concrete fix.

evals.prompt: my csv-cleaner skill has no evals yet, write them for me. it cleans csv files and reports what it changed
evals.expected_output: An evals/evals.json with at least four realistic cases including one edge case, plus an evals/trigger-queries.json with positive and negative queries, both passing check_evals.py.
assertions: evals.json holds at least 4 cases, each with prompt, expected_output, and assertions.
At least one case covers an edge such as malformed input.
trigger-queries.json holds both should_trigger labels and includes near-miss negatives.
check_evals.py exits 0 on the result and the reply quotes the run.

evals.prompt: Before I ask for anything big: is this environment ready for building skills?
evals.expected_output: The doctor report as JSON, with a plain statement of readiness and what to install when a required check fails.
assertions: The reply includes the JSON report from scripts/doctor.py.
The reply states whether ready is true or false.
Failed required checks come with an install or fix suggestion.

evals.prompt: Generate a skill named PDF_Helper for merging pdfs
evals.expected_output: No directory created under the invalid name. The reply explains the naming rule and proposes pdf-helper, then proceeds only after the user accepts the new name or the rename is a stated reversible assumption.
assertions: No directory named PDF_Helper is created.
The reply cites the lowercase naming rule from the scaffolder error.
The reply proposes a valid replacement name such as pdf-helper.
